creator: kseniia.journey-london-rexby
caption:
2 Days, 2 Adventures: A guide to exploring different sides of Singapore🌟
 
City in Nature day:
🌿 Start your morning with a hike at Southern Ridges—the city views are absolutely breathtaking, and take a cable car ride with a glass floor for a unique vantage point

☕ Head to Arbora @ Mount Faber for a coffee and delicious treats
🎨 In the afternoon, explore Gillman Barracks, a hidden gem filled with contemporary art galleries, cafés, and restaurants
🍽️ Finish the day with a memorable dining experience at Restaurant Air—perfect for a special evening out
 
Cultural and retail experience:
🥞Enjoy some freshly made crispy prata from Mr & Mrs Mohgan’s Super Crispy Roti Prata—it’s a must-try!
☕ Grab a mango-flavoured coffee at Kohi Roastery and try rice dumplings from Rumah Kim Choo—two hidden gems you won’t want to miss!
🛍️Shop for unique finds at Cat Socrates, a cosy spot for local treasures
🍴End your day at East Coast Lagoon Food Village, where you can dive into the vibrant local food market
 
My favourite discovery over these two days was Henderson Waves which was part of my hike at the Southern Ridges. It stands at 36 m above Henderson Road, making it the highest pedestrian bridge in Singapore!
 
Save this itinerary for your next trip! You won’t regret exploring these amazing spots in Singapore✈️✨

transcription.text: I curated a two-day guide exploring different sides of Singapore. For a city in nature day, start with a hike at Southern Ridges for stunning city views. And take a cable car ride with a glass floor for a unique vantage point. Then grab a coffee and some delicious snacks from Arbora and Mount Faber. Before heading to Gilman Barracks, a hidden gem filled with contemporary art and great cafes. For dinner, try the Farm to Table Restaurant Air offering a seasonal menu with unique flavors. For a cultural and retail experience, start the next day with a mango coffee from Kohirossery. And enjoy some freshly made crispy prata from Mr. and Mrs. Morgan for breakfast. I also recommend trying rice dumplings at Rumma Kimchiu. The presentation really surprised me and they offer many flavors to choose from. Then stop by the Cat Socrates shop to look for special souvenirs and gifts. At the end of the day, head to East Coast Lagoon Food Village where you will find flavorful street food. And I really enjoyed drinking straight from the coconut which was very refreshing. My favorite discovery over these two days was Henderson Waves, part of my Southern Ridges hike. At 36 meters above Henderson Road, it's the highest pedestrian bridge in Singapore. Make sure to save these locations for your next trip.
